item.title: French Open bans cameras in player areas after Melbourne criticism
item.description: Organisers of the French Open have promised that their players will enjoy camera-free privacy in a space of their own, following criticism the Australian Open copped from top stars this year.

item.title: National Indigenous Cricket Championships offers pathways for young players
item.description: The National Indigenous Cricket Championships, held in Mackay for the second year, has become one of the key development pathways for young talent.

item.title: Bulldogs cop injury carnage on 'traumatic' night as superstar Darcy tears ACL
item.description: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS.

item.title: Middle East conflict creates volatile airfare market as prices fluctuate
item.description: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: LIV Golf facing funding fight to 'keep us going', CEO Scott O'Neil admits in deleted interview
item.description: The future of LIV Golf remains in doubt as chief executive Scott O'Neil admits in an interview that the league is "funded through the season" but work is required beyond that "to keep us going".

item.title: Haas expected to miss several weeks as Broncos count cost of brave win
item.description: Brisbane's horror run of injuries has continued with Payne Haas limping off in the first half of the Broncos' one-point win over Wests Tigers.

item.title: The Solomons Six were recruited to a 'catch and kill' squad in WWII — their stories were lost to history until now
item.description: In the 1940s, a shadowy military unit was assembled to catch and kill Japanese soldiers on Australia's remote northern coast. The identity of the so-called Solomons Six was a mystery — until now.

item.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
item.description: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

item.title: Couple makes handmade skis out of Australian-grown timber in NSW Snowy Mountains
item.description: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

item.title: Love story: I fell in love with my daughter's father two years after she was born
item.description: After a casual fling led to a surprise pregnancy in my mid-20s, I was parenting alone. A phone call two years later changed everything.

item.title: Eels stun Bulldogs after having spirit and desire publicly questioned
item.description: One week after their coach publicly questioned their spirit and desire, Parramatta produces a boilover to beat the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs.

item.title: Newcastle Jets claim maiden A-League Men's premiership after Central Coast shocks Auckland
item.description: Newcastle has claimed a maiden A-League Men's Premiers Plate after local rivals Central Coast stunned second-placed Auckland.

item.title: With Iran and the Strait of Hormuz still in flux, China's bet on renewable energy is paying off
item.description: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy, China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

item.title: Peter and Rosie use power of song and an appearance on The Piano to save endangered language
item.description: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

item.title: Public health researcher David Thomas wants the NT to introduce laws tackling illicit tobacco
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to shortages caused by war in Iran
item.description: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

item.title: Cameron Green victim of miracle stumping in IPL, Cooper Connolly powers Punjab Kings to another win
item.description: The losing run for Cameron Green's Kolkata in the IPL finally ends, but the Australian is undone by a spectacular stumping from Dhruv Jurel, while compatriot Cooper Connolly explodes for the Punjab Kings.

item.title: Grocery prices set to surge due to war in Iran
item.description: Listen to ABC News interviews and commentary and analysis from radio programs like AM, PM and The World Today.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: How many games will be shown on free-to-air TV in the Nine broadcast deal?
item.description: Uncertainties over the details of Netball Australia's broadcast agreement with Nine left fans hesitant to celebrate. Now we reveal how many games will actually be seen on free-to-air TV.

item.title: Potentially 800,000 year old Australite found in WA Goldfields
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed
item.description: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

item.title: Humanoid robots race in Beijing's robot half-marathon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Carlton confirms Elijah Hollands is in hospital after concerning scenes on Thursday night
item.description: Carlton says young star Elijah Hollands has been admitted to hospital after suffering a mental health episode in Thursday night's match.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama's post-season entrance among leading storylines as 2026 NBA playoffs begin
item.description: The first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs is officially underway. Here are the key things to look out for in each series.

item.title: Woolworths fronts court on dodgy discount allegations, ASX slips — as it happened
item.description: Supermarket giant Woolworths faced off with the consumer watchdog in court on Tuesday. The ASX 200 slipped into the red. Catch up on the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Former civil servant says there was political pressure to approve Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US
item.description: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

item.title: Narco Islands: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Whether it's grief or liberation, family estrangement affects many of us
item.description: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here's how some Australians have been affected by it.

item.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
item.description: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

item.title: ASX tumbles as Cochlear shares crash 40pc to 10-year low — as it happened
item.description: One of Australia's biggest companies, Cochlear, has rattled investors by slashing its profit outlook, while the broader market drops.

item.title: Australian golf junior hoping to test his skills at Canadian invitational
item.description: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

item.title: Waste-to-energy plant proposed for Vuda Point in Fiji sparks fierce backlash
item.description: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

item.title: Fuel crisis making access to healthcare prohibitively expensive in regional areas
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Bikie kingpin's private phone calls exposed in Mongols drug extortion case
item.description: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina hits world ranking low after early Madrid Open exit
item.description: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

item.title: Netflix starts filming series Breakers in Western Australia's Wheatbelt
item.description: There is a buzz about town in a small community north of Perth as filming begins for a new Netflix series.

item.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands
item.description: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

item.title: Authors ditch University of Queensland Press after Indigenous kids book pulled for 'anti-Semitism' of illustrator
item.description: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

item.title: Grapevine berry inner necrosis virus officially detected for first time in multiple Australian states
item.description: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O counter-sued by radio show broadcaster
item.description: The company that broadcast the Kyle and Jackie O Show has launched a counter claim against the duo seeking damages for breach of contract, losses in profit and advertising revenue, and legal costs.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Britain bracing for prospect of sixth PM in seven years, as some say Keir Starmer's leadership 'now terminal'
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

item.title: Wallaroos ready to face New Zealand in Anzac Day Test after wild weather on United States tour
item.description: Having survived the threat of tornadoes during their US tour, the Wallaroos are enjoying a distraction-free build-up to their Anzac Day Test against New Zealand.

item.title: Tasmania Police sergeant used dispatch system in 'relentless' monitoring of wife in family violence matter
item.description: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

item.title: Adelaide Festival appoints new Writers' Week director in wake of crisis
item.description: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

item.title: The 10 best books out in April, featuring Patrick Radden Keefe and Caro Claire Burke
item.description: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

item.title: Yamatji Vietnam veteran Graham Taylor remains brothers in arms with platoon mates from 1971
item.description: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

item.title: 100-year-old bugler prepares for Anzac Day ceremonies in regional Victoria town
item.description: A Boort centenarian prepares to sound his bugle this Anzac Day at local schools and the RSL.

item.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
item.description: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands returns to court over termination of ARN Media contract
item.description: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson have separately launched legal proceedings following the termination of their $100 million contracts.

item.title: Injury-decimated Broncos score spectacular tries to heap more pain on Dogs
item.description: Injury-hit Brisbane stands tall at a wet and wild Lang Park, scoring two remarkable tries in a 32-12 defeat of Canterbury-Bankstown.

item.title: India wants to redraw its electoral map for the first time in decades, but could this marginalise its southern states?
item.description: India's government proposed a plan to get more women into parliament but it came with one major condition.

item.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care at Modbury Hospital
item.description: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Former high-profile NT doctor committed to stand trial on multiple sexual offences, including rape
item.description: A former high-profile Northern Territory doctor charged with multiple sexual offences, including rape, has been committed to stand trial later this year.

item.title: With Mojtaba Khamenei a 'symbolic sovereign', who is running Iran?
item.description: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

item.title: How the NDIS grew to four times the size that was expected
item.description: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

item.title: The outcome of the Farrer by-election could be a watershed moment in Australian politics
item.description: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

item.title: James Valentine loved us in our most ridiculous moments
item.description: As an aspiring broadcaster, I covertly watched everything James Valentine did on air. He taught me how in the absurd minutia of our lives, we reveal ourselves to each other.

item.title: Surfer finds new life for decommissioned wind turbine blades
item.description: At the Waubra Wind Farm near Ballarat, 40-metre turbine blades that once helped power the state are given a second life.

item.title: Young Indigenous artist uses work to explore personal stories, experiences in care system
item.description: An Indigenous teenager is already making a name for herself as an artist, using her work to explore her identity and experiences in the care system.

item.title: Alex de Minaur beaten by Rafael Jodar in second round of Madrid Masters
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses another early-round match at the Madrid Masters, going down to local teen Rafael Jodar in straight sets as his clay court season struggles.

item.title: Bushfire survivors build fire-resilient house into Buchan hillside
item.description: During the Black Summer bushfires, the Grahams watched their house burn from a peephole in a fire bunker. When they decided to rebuild, they created the most fire-resilient home possible. Here's how they did it.

item.title: Indigenous solar projects in Borroloola and Santa Teresa to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills
item.description: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

item.title: Voluntary assisted dying advocates credit awareness raised by James Valentine and his family
item.description: For someone who revelled in celebrating humanity, one of James Valentine's final wishes was to make it publicly known his choice to access NSW's VAD laws.

item.title: Nelly Korda streaks ahead in Chevron Championship second round as Hannah Green sneaks under the cut
item.description: The Australian charge is struggling at the Chevron Championship in Texas, where world number two Nelly Korda is on fire after two rounds.

item.title: Cotton industry 'alert but not alarmed' by increasing numbers of bicoloured weevils in NT crops
item.description: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

item.title: How Henry the dachshund puppy survived three months lost in Perth's southern suburbs
item.description: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

item.title: Donald Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as Middle East peace hopes fade
item.description: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Sex workers speak out about the HIV crisis in Papua New Guinea
item.description: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

item.title: NDIS cuts show when Labor will move, gas tax shows when it won't
item.description: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

item.title: Gippsland footballer with Guillain-Barré syndrome returns to field after induced coma
item.description: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

item.title: Dragons halfback Kade Reed to keep starting role, says interim coach Dean Young
item.description: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

item.title: Ollie Hollands a late out for Carlton after Elijah Hollands's struggles, but coach Michael Voss says he is 'in a good space'
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

item.title: Predator-free farm helps endangered species thrive
item.description: A predator-proof fence at a Victorian farm is helping endangered species recover while proving conservation and agriculture can successfully co-exist.

item.title: Queensland's remote national park islands where you can volunteer and holiday for free
item.description: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

item.title: Ancient, critically endangered 'sterile' plant King's lomatia found for second time in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

item.title: Thirty years after losing his family in the Port Arthur massacre, Walter Mikac says gun control is still vital
item.description: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

item.title: Seed swaps, preserved food and community gardens as preppers brace for uncertain future
item.description: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

item.title: Preparing food for future use with a pressure cooker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Indonesian farmers improve food security while protecting Australia from citrus disease
item.description: An Australian-funded project is educating Indonesian farmers about a devastating citrus disease that has spread to all major growing regions except Australia.

item.title: Why Shaun Micallef wrote 'gothic romance novel' De'Ath Takes a Holiday
item.description: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

item.title: Nelly Korda ties Chevron Championship's 54-hole record despite late struggles in third round
item.description: World number two Nelly Korda ties a Chevron Championship record as she takes a five-stroke lead into the final round of the year's first women's golf major.

item.title: Melbourne on track to record its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade
item.description: A blocking high has put Melbourne on track for its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade.

item.title: Resources industry in the spotlight during Angus Taylor's first trip to WA as Opposition leader
item.description: This past week marks the first time the Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Taylor and Premier Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

item.title: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild on the Coorong, but scientists are hunting for more
item.description: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

item.title: Tasmanian coastal community of Dolphin Sands on bushfire watch again
item.description: A coastal community recently ravaged by a bushfire has again been on alert in Tasmania.

item.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after fine threat for using accessible zone without permit
item.description: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

item.title: Man in custody after allegedly assaulting several people including nurses at Randwick's Prince of Wales Hospital
item.description: A 51-year-old man seriously injured a nurse's leg at Prince of Wales Hospital in Randwick about 8:30pm on Saturday night, before assaulting two other nurses and a security guard.

item.title: Veteran's advice after Anzac Day booing: Find your 'warrior spirit'
item.description: While condemnation has been swift after Indigenous elders were booed on Anzac Day, solutions to this type of racism seem harder to pin down. Elders have advice to share.

item.title: Why women are getting less sleep than men — and what we can do about it
item.description: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

item.title: Donald Trump was 'all set to really rip' into the media, but shooting may help change his tune
item.description: While the latest act of violence wasn't as close as Trump's brush with death in Butler, Pennsylvania, almost two years ago, it will no doubt revive some trauma for Trump and those closest to him.

item.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players
item.description: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

item.title: Donald Trump says Washington shooter's 'manifesto' shows he is a 'sick guy'
item.description: Several US media outlets are reporting on the existence of the manifesto, which reportedly states the shooter wanted to target administration officials and was sent to the suspect's family members just before the attack.

item.title: The 'groundbreaking' AI tool helping Victorian rangers protect native species in a fraction of the time
item.description: A new AI tool will help Parks Victoria manage native and endangered species affected by natural disasters, including the summer bushfires, with the department making the technology available to everyone.

item.title: Platypuses need threatened species recognition in NSW, researcher says
item.description: A leading researcher is calling for the platypus to be recognised as a threatened species in NSW, saying a lack of data is the only reason it has not been reclassified.

item.title: AI decision review system being trialled in Darwin women's division one cricket competition
item.description: Darwin's premier women's cricket league becomes the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

item.title: Wet week ahead for south-east Qld due to unusually 'persistent' weather system
item.description: The "very slow-moving" high-pressure system causing the wet weather should "finally get a move on" early next week, the BOM says.

item.title: Fuel prices create anxiety for Queensland's community events, but the show must go on
item.description: The nation's fuel crisis continues to hamper community events across regional Queensland, as organisers grapple with soaring costs.

item.title: Canberra is growing its economic 'pie' to become more than a city of public administration
item.description: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

item.title: Esteban Andrada punches Jorge Pulido, sparking Aragonese derby brawl between Real Zaragoza and Huesca
item.description: The Aragonese derby between Real Zaragoza and Huesca descends into a wild brawl after Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punches Huesca skipper Jorge Pulido in the face.

item.title: How four remote Queensland farms became the oil capital of Australia
item.description: With oil exploration back in the headlines, here's how Australia struck it lucky in the 1960s.

item.title: Mother calls for a more accessible world after daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis
item.description: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

item.title: WA Criminal Mental Impairment Act 'impossible' to fully implement in practice, judge says
item.description: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

item.title: Japan's north struck by magnitude-6.1 earthquake, followed by wildfires prompting evacuations
item.description: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

item.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla proceed with US state visit after White House shooting
item.description: The British monarch and Queen Camilla are embarking on a state visit to the United States with relations between the two countries strained and following a shooting at a press gala in Washington.

item.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it
item.description: Sabastian Sawe smashes one of athletics' seemingly unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

item.title: Australian Gregory Louden on leading PlayStation's next big title, Saros
item.description: Developer Gregory Louden always wanted to work in games, but it was a sidestep into filmmaking that helped him secure his dream job at Finnish studio Housemarque.

item.title: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission
item.description: Orthopaedic surgeon Herwig Drobetz returns to Gaza for fourth mercy mission.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: PNG prime minister encouraged by Jarome Luai's visit and meeting with Chiefs bosses
item.description: The Wests Tigers playmaker is the Chiefs' top target ahead of their NRL entry in 2028, and the Papua New Guinea prime minister says his trip to PNG shows the interest is mutual.

item.title: Indigenous veteran Di Ryder slams 'distracting' booing at Anzac Day Welcome to Country
item.description: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

item.title: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas says algal bloom 'gone' but fishing restrictions need to expand
item.description: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australia due to the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks, but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up".

item.title: Four easy egg recipes for any occasion
item.description: If you're looking to add the humble egg into your weekly meals, we have you covered.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: The moment Andy Lee feared exploits with Hamish Blake had gone too far
item.description: Andy Lee opens up on his 23-year working relationship with his "fast and loose" comedy partner Hamish Blake, and the stunt that left him fearing for his mate's life.

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Apple's Tim Cook another casualty as worries about AI's impact on jobs grow
item.description: Tim Cook helped usher in a communications revolution and his departure from Apple marks a turning point for the company. It also serves as a reminder of how AI is changing the tech landscape.

item.title: Health insurer Bupa accused of anti-competitive behaviour under secretive No Gap scheme deals
item.description: Leaked contracts obtained by the ABC reveal Bupa's deals with private hospitals impose stifling conditions on operators that are too small to push back.

item.title: Australian rugby may benefit from copying Crusaders' lateral thinking with Leicester Fainga'anuku
item.description: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

item.title: Scenic Rim community divided over proposed Cleanaway waste-to-energy incinerator
item.description: A proposed waste-to-energy incinerator near Beaudesert divides the Scenic Rim community. Supporters say it could ease Queensland's landfill crisis, while opponents fear health and environmental risks.

item.title: Farmer rescues kangaroo from mud in dry dam as drought conditions hit western NSW
item.description: Photos show a kangaroo moments before being rescued from a dam in Central West NSW, after it became bogged in mud while searching for water in an increasingly barren landscape.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: New York mayor announces free FIFA World Cup fan zones as costs continue to escalate
item.description: New York City will host free events for soccer fans who can't afford the hefty ticket prices for the FIFA World Cup, Mayor Zohran Mamdani says.

item.title: Push to make voluntary assisted dying available through telehealth
item.description: Despite voluntary assisted dying being legal in Victoria, many regional patients are not getting the same end-of-life care.

item.title: Trump greets King Charles at White House
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NFL kicker Younghoe Koo's comical error revealed a brain tumour in fan Mark Toothaker
item.description: Mark Toothaker laughed so hard at Younghoe Koo's botched NFL kick that he had a seizure. It may have saved his life.

item.title: Indigenous players defend St Kilda coach Ross Lyon after report reveals comment during training session
item.description: Several indigenous players who have played under Saints coach Ross Lyon come to his defence after comments he made during a training session led to a meeting where Lyon offered to stand down.

item.title: Donald Trump calls for broadcaster to fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Broken PBS system behind failure to list Mounjaro for type 2 diabetics, according to Medicines Australia
item.description: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

item.title: Rebel Wilson describes herself as 'champion of women' as actor gives evidence in defamation trial
item.description: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: Former Australian federal commissioner Tom Rogers to lead independent review into SA election
item.description: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

item.title: Tasmanian teachers strike three-year deal to eradicate violence and lessen workload in schools
item.description: After months of dispute, an agreement has been reached on Tasmanian teachers' pay and conditions for the next three years.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Yarra River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Single-use plastic soy sauce fish are an eco-nightmare. A local designer has a solution
item.description: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

item.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
item.description: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

item.title: Millie Elliott returns after having a baby to line up for NSW in State of Origin Game I
item.description: Millie Elliott could have taken an easy route back into competitive action after having a baby. Instead, she is throwing herself straight back into the State of Origin cauldron on Thursday night.

item.title: Health insurers accused of 'aggressive' and 'almost bullying' tactics in private hospital dealings
item.description: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

item.title: Donald Trump names former Republican congressman as new US ambassador to Australia
item.description: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

item.title: Mother 'always thinks' about whether birth delay at regional hospital led to son's ongoing health issues
item.description: A regional South Australian mother who faced a range of stressful diversions during her son's birth says she will always wonder whether his serious and ongoing health concerns were caused by delays in receiving care.

item.title: Locusts seen on Riverland farm
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as Australian plague locust migrates to South Australia
item.description: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

item.title: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel set to go under the hammer, but no inspections allowed
item.description: Rockhampton's Plaza Hotel is set to go under the hammer after closing down more than a decade ago.

item.title: New police operation investigating crime syndicates 'pulling the strings' in Melbourne firebombings
item.description: Police are investigating whether recent arson attacks across 15 Melbourne hospitality venues are linked to an organised crime syndicate.

item.title: NuGrow to fight charges of nuisance odours from Ipswich facility
item.description: Waste recycling company NuGrow will fight 11 environmental nuisance charges relating to its Swanbank food recycling plant in a two-week trial later this year.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Penny Wong to press Asian countries for fuel guarantees during regional tour
item.description: As Australia grapples with energy supply issues, Penny Wong travels to Japan to stress the importance of an ongoing supply of fuel.

item.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
item.description: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Netball's powerful all-female statement on Anzac Day
item.description: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

item.title: Man pleads guilty in Austria to plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
item.description: A 21-year-old man has pleaded guilty over a jihadist plot to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Austria, which led to three of the star's shows being cancelled in 2024.

item.title: Alleged gunman, 89, arrested after injuring five in Athens shootings
item.description: The elderly man opened fire at a social security agency and then a courthouse in Athens before fleeing the scene, police say.

item.title: Superyacht linked to Putin's friend slips through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It's the most talked-about and dangerous stretch of ocean in the world but, at the weekend, a superyacht linked to one of Russia's richest men made it through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UAE leaves OPEC in major blow to global oil producers' group
item.description: It comes as unprecedented energy crisis triggered by the Iran war exposes splits among Gulf nations.

item.title: Live: King Charles says 'acts of violence will never succeed' after attack on Trump
item.description: King Charles has delivered a speech to Congress as part of his visit to the US to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence. Follow live.

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: First Nations film Wolfram writes hope into heavy history
item.description: Convinced to return to the story of the 2018 outback drama Sweet Country, Kaytetye filmmaker Warwick Thornton fell in love with the possibilities.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 29: Grab your ticket and a map, we're off on a quiz adventure
item.description: The Galápagos Islands are part of which South American country? The Elgin Marbles were taken from which ancient site?

item.title: Hundreds of child abuse files found on devices belonging to police killer Dezi Freeman
item.description: Police killer Dezi Freeman was on the verge of having his secret stash of child abuse material discovered when he gunned down two officers outside his makeshift home.

item.title: Australia lacks national strategy to regulate AI spread in workplace, report states
item.description: Australia risks repeating the mistakes it made with social media unless it moves quickly to regulate the spread of artificial intelligence in the workplace, according to a new report.

item.title: Plastic packaging price pain could push up costs for shoppers on everything from milk to make-up
item.description: Companies that use plastic packaging are facing price pressures and supply disruptions, which they warn will likely mean higher costs for consumers. The cost of food-grade resin out of Singapore, which is used in milk bottles, has increased 110 per cent since the start of the Middle East war.

item.title: Amount of antibiotic florfenicol used at Tasmanian salmon farm lease concerning, say Greens, expert
item.description: Almost 3 tonnes of the antibiotic florfenicol were used at one Tasmanian salmon farm lease in just two months, with information on its use across eight other sites yet to be released to the public by authorities.

item.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
item.description: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

item.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?
item.description: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: Former ASIO chief questioned scrutiny of Bondi gunmen before quitting royal commission
item.description: While advising the royal commission examining the Bondi Beach terrorist attack, the former head of Australia's domestic intelligence agency questioned whether early assessments of gunman Naveed Akram were sufficiently rigorous.

item.title: SEQ first-home buyers turn to Melbourne as 5% Deposit Scheme provides limited opportunity
item.description: South-East Queensland has been under-represented in 5% Deposit Scheme approvals, with prospective first-home buyers heading to Melbourne for its "considerable affordability" benefits.

item.title: Live: US Marines board suspected Iran blockade runner, Israel hits Gaza, Lebanon
item.description: US President Donald Trump says Iranian leadership has told him they are in a "state of collapse" and are urging him to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as US marines board cargo ships in the region and Israeli strikes kill three in Lebanon. Follow live.

item.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
item.description: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

item.title: Western NSW dam limit increase plan dumped over impact on protected wetlands
item.description: Water users in part of the Murray-Darling Basin are anxious that a proposal to protect their interests during droughts has been abandoned, as the dry conditions continue to worsen.

item.title: What we know about the night 5yo girl Sharon Granites went missing in Alice Springs
item.description: Five-year-old Sharon Granites vanished from an Alice Springs town camp late on Saturday night, with police alleging she has been abducted. Here's what we know about the night she went missing.

item.title: King Charles addresses joint sitting of US Congress
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Early Farrer voters make choices known as pre-polling gets underway in blockbuster by-election
item.description: Pre-polling commences in the seat of Farrer for the hotly contested by-election triggered by the resignation of former opposition leader Sussan Ley.

item.title: Can the SA government justify a $45m golf course upgrade without LIV Golf?
item.description: The Malinauskas government took a calculated risk to redevelop the North Adelaide Golf Course in time for LIV Golf in 2028. It might not pay off.

item.title: Self-employment can help autistic people avoid workplace barriers
item.description: After the National Autism Strategy identifies the potential of self-employment to provide meaningful work for people with autism, neurodivergent people who are already self-employed say it can help overcome workplace barriers.

item.title: Paid advertisements used to promote fraudulent investment platforms on social media, warns ASIC
item.description: The Sydney dad believed he was investing in cryptocurrency, stocks and even gold with the hopes of buying a home.

item.title: Marcus Stoinis hits half-century in Punjab Kings' six-wicket IPL loss to Rajasthan Royals
item.description: Ricky Ponting's Punjab Kings record a six-wicket loss against Rajasthan Royals in the IPL, despite Marcus Stoinis's breathtaking innings.

item.title: Live: PM to announce $45 million to fast-track housing, energy projects
item.description: The prime minister will announce $45 million to help speed housing and energy project approvals. Follow live.

item.title: Why WA's unique history has it leading the pack on ADHD medication prescriptions
item.description: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

item.title: Takeaways from King Charles's speech to the US Congress
item.description: In a speech that was supposed to be apolitical, King Charles hits on some hot-button issues that are fuelling controversies around the Trump administration.

item.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
item.description: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their key off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: Live: Inflation likely to surge, ASX to drop for seventh straight day as UAE quits OPEC
item.description: The ABS is set to release data that is likely to show inflation spiking at about 4.8 per cent in the March quarter, its highest level in two and a half years. The RBA is widely expected to lift interest rates again next week.

item.title: Family of Queensland grandmother who died after weight-loss surgery claims her death was preventable
item.description: Joanne Downie underwent an elective gastric bypass surgery so she could live a healthier life for her grandchildren, but instead she died six days later in a regional Queensland hospital.

item.title: Victorian opposition promises new cap on public sector executive pay
item.description: The Coalition is promising to cap the salaries of senior public sector executives at just under $600,000 a year to "restore financial discipline".

item.title: LIV Golf postpones United States event amid tour's uncertain future
item.description: LIV Golf and the US state of Louisiana make a call on the tour's event in New Orleans, which was slated for June.

item.title: UK parliament votes against inquiry into Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over Peter Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer criticised the vote, calling it a political stunt by the Conservative Party timed to sway voters ahead of local and regional elections on May 7.

item.title: Australia pushed for stronger gun laws in the wake of the Bondi massacre. So where are they all up to?
item.description: Firearms reforms were swift and lasting after Port Arthur. But Bondi has proved a far more challenging political process for the federal government. So where are we at with the gun law reforms?

item.title: A man sets fire to a Pilates studio on the NSW Central Coast.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Video of arson attack on Central Coast Pilates studio released by police
item.description: The video released by police captures the moment a man douses the Pilates studio in petrol before setting it alight.

item.title: VicGrid surveyors cancel access visit after farmgate protest makes them feel unsafe
item.description: Renewable planning body VicGrid has threatened a high-profile anti-transmission-line campaigner with enforcement action after government surveyors felt "hindered" from accessing his farm.

item.title: US regulator orders review of ABC licence after Donald Trump criticises Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: It comes after both Donald and Melania Trump demanded the network cancel Kimmel's late-night comedy show over a joke they described as a call to violence, days before an alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

item.title: Jarome Luai confirms Wests Tigers exit to join PNG Chiefs for 2028 NRL season
item.description: Jarome Luai will leave Wests Tigers at the end of the 2027 season, announcing he will join the PNG Chiefs for their entry to the NRL.

item.title: Australian actor Megan Wilding stars in Game. Set. Match, a play that feels like tennis
item.description: What starts as a rom-com set at the Australian Open turns into a searing indictment of race relations in Australia in the new show Game. Set. Match.

item.title: Jarome Luai's move to Papua New Guinea makes the power of the Chiefs very real
item.description: Jarome Luai's decision to sign with Papua New Guinea is a showcase of the economic power behind the Chiefs and marks the start of the greatest rugby league gold rush since Super League.

item.title: New theatre hopes to attract audiences as remote Queensland town grapples with tourism crisis
item.description: A potentially 'catastrophic' drop in traffic has outback residents concerned for the rest of the tourist season. Community members in remote Queensland have come together for the grand opening of a new outdoor theatre and a ribbon-cutting ceremony, declaring their town open for business.
